# Questions in Livestream 222

Live Stream #222: The Master of All Classes

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=628" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">10:28</a> | Does the Red Sea crossing justify infant baptism? | He does not think that is a strong argument and says the analogy to circumcision is too weak. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=920" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">15:20</a> | When Hebrew dropped the ghayin sound, how was the pronunciation preserved in Gaza? | He says the local place-name tradition preserved it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=1040" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">17:20</a> | How would David answer radical pacifists who say Jesus never talks about war in the New Testament? | He calls that an argument from silence and not enough to mandate absolute pacifism. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=1332" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">22:12</a> | Did your Mennonite friends reject self-defense, or only attacking first? | He says many of them rejected aggression but still allowed self-defense. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=1495" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">24:55</a> | Aren't Mennonites anti-alcohol? | No; he says they are not anti-alcohol. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=1696" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">28:16</a> | Why are people's heads shown in profile rather than front view in ancient art? | He ties that mainly to the limits and habits of the artistic media being used. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=2030" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">33:50</a> | How are geophysical survey methods such as seismic resistance and GPR used in ancient Near Eastern archaeology? | He says GPR and cesium magnetometry are important and very effective, while seismic and resistance are less central. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=2369" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">39:29</a> | What was ancient Egypt's relationship with cats? | Cats were pets, village animals, hunting aids, and religiously associated with Bast or Bastet. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=2720" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">45:20</a> | Why does my profile picture look bad when you highlight my comments on stream? | He says the masking and overlay setup in OBS is the cause. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=2991" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">49:51</a> | Who was the most incompetent or bafflingly stupid pharaoh in Egyptian history? | He nominates Ptolemy XIII. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=3121" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">52:01</a> | Do we know when Amunherkhepshef's firstborn died? | He says the exact year is uncertain, though he gives a rough Ramses II range. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=3306" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">55:06</a> | Can you recap the Matthew and Luke genealogy question and who the Hyksos were? | He says Matthew gives legal descent through Joseph, Luke gives Christ's genetic descent, and the Hyksos were Amorite Semites who formed Egypt's Fifteenth Dynasty. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=3415" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">56:55</a> | How can I tell whether a college is a degree mill? | Check accreditation, academic requirements, oversight, and whether it appears on known degree-mill lists. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=4497" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:14:57</a> | Is Dr. Faulk a fan of Dune? | Yes; he says he is a big fan of Frank Herbert's original six-book series. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=4781" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:19:41</a> | What does ABR stand for? | It stands for Associates for Biblical Research. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=5019" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:23:39</a> | Is dealing with ABR or Rohl supporters more insufferable? | He says both are bad, but ABR has more institutional and financial power. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=5207" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:26:47</a> | Are David Down's followers less vocal? | He says they are now largely absent because Down died years ago and his views have faded. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=5242" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:27:22</a> | Is there a sound way to detect oxymorons in the Old Testament? | He does not know of an easy or reliable method apart from close reading. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=5312" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:28:32</a> | Why is pride so irresistible for believers and non-believers? | He says sinful pride feels superior, distorts self-assessment, and corrodes community. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=5782" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:36:22</a> | Was throwing the food away just for show? | Yes; he says it preserved the recipient's dignity while still getting him food. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=5979" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:39:39</a> | What are your thoughts on the Frankfurt silver inscription? | He says it appears legitimate, comes from a real excavation, and is important early evidence of Christianity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=6176" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:42:56</a> | Could the banana farm on top of Avaris be bought out for archaeology? | He says Avaris is partly accessible through field rentals, but he would especially like to excavate the plantation over the likely Migdol area. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=6388" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:46:28</a> | Does Egypt have eminent domain like the United States? | Yes; he says Egypt has eminent domain, though it is used more for state projects and urgent rescue archaeology than ordinary digs. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=6468" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:47:48</a> | Do we know where the Lion's Den is? | He identifies the fortress called the Lion's Den with Tell el-Borg on the Road of Horus. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=6541" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:49:01</a> | Was firstborn inheritance automatic in the ancient Near East, and what happened if an Egyptian father had only daughters? | He says Egyptian inheritance centered on a designated male inheritor and job titles, but women and daughters could inherit under certain family circumstances. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=6897" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:54:57</a> | Did the wife have to bear a son to receive her husband's inheritance? | No; he says bearing any child could make her claim permanent. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=7114" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:58:34</a> | Is Egyptian inheritance why God intervened with the daughters of Zelophehad? | Yes; he says they needed special dispensation because Israel was operating with Egyptian inheritance expectations. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=7154" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:59:14</a> | When God judges a sinful nation, are repentant believers within it spared? | Not necessarily; he says believers can still suffer collateral damage from the sins of their community. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=8057" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:14:17</a> | Where do you get your recipes from? | He says he uses many sources, including cookbooks, online recipes, and family recipes. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=8140" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:15:40</a> | Is fish also expensive in Vancouver? | Yes; he says fish is expensive there despite Vancouver being a port city. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=8175" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:16:15</a> | Do you like hibachi? | He likes the taste but not the cleanup. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=8233" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:17:13</a> | What is hibachi? | He describes it as a small Japanese coal-based grill used for quick searing. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=8348" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:19:08</a> | Are you allowed to air fry on keto? | Yes; he says there is nothing wrong with air frying on keto. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=8365" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:19:25</a> | If someone is not a Christian and does not know where to go, what should they do? | He says they can go directly to Christ, repent, seek forgiveness, and then work out baptism and church afterward. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=8508" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:21:48</a> | What current academic work or recent digs can you share? | He says he is not doing digs now, plans to work on the Exodus book, and recently published on computational chronologies. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=8609" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:23:29</a> | What is your biggest accomplishment in archaeology? | He names his book on the Ark of the Covenant in its Egyptian context. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=8843" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:27:23</a> | What wine would you recommend for fish? | He recommends a white Bordeaux for cod and a rose or white zinfandel for smoked salmon. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=8998" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:29:58</a> | What wine works with a charcuterie board? | He recommends a red Bordeaux or Beaujolais because the tannins pair well with the board. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=9246" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:34:06</a> | Can wine be too aged? | Yes; he says over-aged wine oxidizes and loses quality, with different aging windows for whites, reds, and roses. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=9426" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:37:06</a> | Are there good tasting wines for someone who dislikes dry, bitter wine? | He suggests starting with lower-tannin wines such as zinfandels and white wines, while noting tastes can change with age. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=9550" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:39:10</a> | Is it possible to be Protestant and believe in the assumption of Mary? | Yes; he says nothing in Protestantism strictly prevents it, though Protestants generally do not believe that dogma. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=9669" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:41:09</a> | Are there good books comparing the cases for a global or regional flood? | He thinks books exist but advises doing a literature review and adding something new if writing on the subject. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=9778" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:42:58</a> | Can AI be used to help with a literature review? | He says he would not use AI for that because it hallucinates and does not read well enough. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=10106" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:48:26</a> | Can writing centers help with literature reviews? | Not generally; he says a literature review is an iterative process of following bibliographies from good sources. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=10357" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:52:37</a> | Didn't Ramses II claim he birthed himself? | No; he says Egyptian divine-birth ideology is not the same as claiming to be self-born. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=10751" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:59:11</a> | Have you made anything functional with Arduino or something similar? | Yes; he uses ESP32s rather than Arduinos, and the cheese cave uses a commercial Inkbird controller. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=10825" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:00:25</a> | Is Canadian bacon actually Canadian? | Yes; he says it is Canadian, though it is basically ham. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=10927" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:02:07</a> | Was Sarah a cruel slave owner, and what is Genesis 16-20 about? | He says Sarah seems cruel toward Hagar, the privilege framing is anachronistic, and the passage shows Abraham and Sarah trying to force prophecy outside God's plan. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=11283" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:08:03</a> | Are the descendants of Isaac and Ishmael still fighting in the Middle East today? | He rejects that as Islamic mythology rather than Jewish or Christian history. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=11444" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:10:44</a> | Why do Arabs hate the Jews? | He gives Islam and the Quran as his direct explanation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=11486" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:11:26</a> | Does your book on the Ark of the Covenant make the ark out to be an idol from a Catholic viewpoint? | No; he says the book treats the ark through Late Bronze Age and early Iron Age cultural context, not Catholic interpretation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=11548" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:12:28</a> | What is the purpose of the biblical writers recording Abraham and Sarah's life? | He says the Abraham narrative frames God's one-sided covenant and promises to his people. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=11745" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:15:45</a> | At Jericho, why were animals inside the city when judgment came? | He says animals were normally brought inside walled cities for safety and for food during a siege. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=11943" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:19:03</a> | What does secular law mean in the Bible, and are there sources to read about it? | He means customary laws that preexisted the covenant and were incorporated into biblical law; he mentions Richard Averbeck and commentaries as places to start. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=12158" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:22:38</a> | Could the springs of the waters of Neftoah in Joshua be a reference to Memphis? | He says the etymology is sound and the suggestion is feasible. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=12239" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:23:59</a> | When is the new covenant declared? | He says it is declared in Jeremiah 31 and consummated through Christ's sacrifice. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=12496" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:28:16</a> | Can an abductive argument for God be made from the Exodus alone? | He dislikes abductive arguments and says an inductive argument from the Exodus evidence would be stronger. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=12774" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:32:54</a> | Can miracles be used as an inductive argument for God? | He says it depends on the class of miracle; second-order miracles of timing are more evidential than first-order miracles that leave no trace. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=12893" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:34:53</a> | What is the biggest problem for a maximalist view of the Exodus and conquest narrative? | He says Moses' identity is a bigger issue than Moses' existence, and extreme maximalism can overread the text. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=13131" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:38:51</a> | Is using indirect evidence the same as using abductive argumentation? | No; he says direct or indirect evidence can support either inductive or abductive arguments, depending on the argumentative structure. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=13520" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:45:20</a> | How strong is the claim that the real Mount Sinai is in Arabia? | He calls the Arabia claim terrible and traces it to bad Ron Wyatt-style theorizing. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=13857" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:50:57</a> | Of the proposed tombs of Jesus, is the Church of the Holy Sepulchre the closest candidate? | Yes; he says the recent archaeological evidence favors it over rivals like Gordon's Calvary. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=14110" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:55:10</a> | Are we assuming there was a garden there because the women thought the angel was a gardener? | Yes; he says you do not have gardeners where there is no garden. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=14165" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:56:05</a> | Was the Wailing Wall part of the Roman fortress Antonia? | No; he says it is part of Herod's retaining structure for the expanded plaza. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=14259" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:57:39</a> | Which tomb do you consider to be David's tomb? | He says he does not know that a securely identified Davidic tomb has survived and notes that Israelite royal burials may have involved cremation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=14468" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:01:08</a> | Would Israelite kings be burned and randomly buried, or would they have family graves? | He says the evidence is uncertain, though Saul may have been buried under an oak or in a sacred grove setting. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=14528" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:02:08</a> | What is the significance of Troy and the Hittites in biblical historical context? | He says the connection is limited, though Troy matters as part of the broader collapse of the Hittite world. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=14705" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:05:05</a> | What are examples of ancient figures whose biographies were written after they died? | He says Roman emperors are an easy example, since later historians often wrote the surviving narratives. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Inh9XfP2T9w?t=15004" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:10:04</a> | How is your plague book coming along? | He says it is not the current project but is next in line after the video game work. |
